title: Horzao Darr

{{Quote|I apologize for the rather abrupt manner you were brought aboard. At first, I believed you to be corsairs from the western canyons. Examination of your unique machines proved this to be an incorrect assumption.|Horzao Darr, to New Republic pilots from the CR90 corvette ''FarStar''|<ref name="TMOE" />}}
'''Horzao Darr''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the isolated [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Exocron]] within the [[Kathol Outback/Legends|Kathol Outback]], a largely unexplored section of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. An [[Officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Exocron Airfleet]], he was a skilled and respected [[sky-sailor]], and [[Commander/Legends|commanded]] the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[sky-dreadnaught]] ''[[Maxion]]''. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Darr made first contact with representatives of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] when the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' accidentally discovered the planet while [[DarkStryder Campaign|searching]] for the rogue [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]. The ruling caste of Darr's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], the [[Devisors]], decreed that the New Republic representatives could never leave Exocron in order to maintain the safety and security of the planet. Disgusted at this turn of events, Darr rescued the stranded crew members and allied with the planet's [[Exocron insurgency|insurgents]] to free the commander of the ''FarStar'', Captain [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]]. This act of defiance would lead to the [[Liberation of Exocron|ending of the Devisors' rule]], freeing the planet from their technocracy.

Darr continued to serve in the military, and by [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]] had been promoted to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Supreme Admiral/Legends|Supreme Admiral]]. He was commander of the [[Exocron Combined Air-Space Fleet]], the successor to the Exocron Airfleet he had served in several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] previously, during the [[Battle of Exocron|attack]] on Exocron by the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Rei'Kas]] and his [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. The assault on the planet was repulsed with the assistance of the [[Aing-Tii/Legends|Aing-Tii]], a [[Species/Legends|race]] of mysterious [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-wielding [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] from the [[Kathol Rift/Legends|Kathol Rift]].

==Biography==
===Early service===
Horzao Darr was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] and raised on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Exocron]], a world hidden in a [[Protostar/Legends|protostar]] [[Gas/Legends|gas]] [[Cloud/Legends|cloud]] in the [[Kathol Outback/Legends|Kathol Outback]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Isolated from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and denied [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel by the ruling caste, the technocratic [[Devisors]], Exocron developed [[Airship/Legends|airships]] for military and economic purposes. At the age of twelve, Darr stowed away aboard a [[sky transport]]; he was discovered, and began his career as a [[sky-sailor]] indentured to the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the vessel to pay for his passage. He learned the skills necessary to be an effective member of the crew, and continued to serve aboard the private vessel for several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] until he enrolled in Exocron's [[Airfleet Academy]]. Darr graduated from the academy with honors, an [[Officer/Legends|officer's]] commission in the [[Exocron Airfleet]], and [[Commander/Legends|command]] of the [[air-cutter]] ''[[Loristar]]''.<ref name="TMOE" />

[[File:Sky-Dreadnaught_Maxion.jpg|thumb|left|The sky-dreadnaught ''Maxion'', flagship of the Exocron Airfleet.]]
During Exocron's [[Exocron's internal conflicts|internal conflicts]], when rebellious factions challenged the Devisors and the [[Ruling council (Exocron)|ruling council]] for control of the planet, Darr commanded the ''Loristar'' on a mission to deliver relief supplies to beleaguered council forces, fighting his way through the enemy to reach them. As a result of his actions, he was given command of the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the Airfleet, the [[sky-dreadnaught]] ''[[Maxion]]''. Darr's new position pulled him into a world of [[Politics/Legends|politics]] and controversy, and several military insiders desired to promote him to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Supreme Admiral/Legends|Supreme Admiral]] and give him command of the Airfleet. They wanted Darr in a position to take power from the council. However, Darr steadfastly resisted all efforts to promote him off the ''Maxion'', preferring to remain in the clouds than in a dank council office.<ref name="TMOE" />

Despite being a loyal officer in the Airfleet, Captain Darr harbored doubts about the Devisors, and felt that they had denied the people of Exocron their birthright. Darr knew that the Devisors concealed the secrets of space travel from the general populace, and felt that they abused their authority by manipulating the ruling council and maintaining absolute power over the planet. This state of affairs had led to rebellion against their rule, ranging from a quiet underground [[Exocron insurgency|insurgency]] to full-blown open aggression in the form of [[Corsairs]] operating out of the planet's [[western canyons]]. Darr kept his feelings hidden, but did know of several people who were involved in the insurgency, such as his friend [[Eida Sharden]]. However, he disagreed with some of the more radical views of the insurgents, and kept them at [[Limb/Legends|arm's]] length.<ref name="TMOE" />

===Arrival of the ''FarStar''===
{{Quote|This world is called Exocron. You are welcome here, as are others of your kind.|Horzao Darr, to New Republic pilots|<ref name="TMOE" />}}
In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],<ref name="Masters-Date">The events of "[[The Masters of Exocron]]" in the ''[[The Kathol Outback]]'' take place four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. The Battle of Endor is stated to be in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] in ''[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|The Essential Chronology]]'', giving a date of [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]].</ref> Darr and the ''Maxion'' intercepted several [[Starfighter/Legends|strange aircraft]] in Exocron's [[Atmosphere/Legends|airspace]]. Initially believing them to be rebellious Corsairs from the western canyons, he ordered them to stand down and land on the ''Maxion'', or they would be destroyed. The craft, [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]] from the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'', complied with Darr's order and put down on the flight deck of the ''Maxion''. Darr met with the [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], learning that they were offworlders and that they were searching for a [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] that they had dispatched to survey the planet as part of their [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]] to track down the rogue [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]. After greeting them formally, Darr apologized for his earlier abrupt manner. He informed his guests that their probe [[Droid/Legends|droid]] had been recovered by a [[Cruiser (Exocron Airfleet)|cruiser]] of the Exocron Airfleet, the ''[[Desaclates]]'', and that he had been ordered to take them to the planetary [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Caballa City]]. Eager to meet with more offworlders, Darr allowed one of the X-wing pilots to return to the ''FarStar'' to inform their superiors of what had occurred, and to invite a delegation down to the ''Maxion''. From there, the ''Maxion'' would transport them to the capital to meet with the Devisors and the ruling council.<ref name="TMOE" />

[[File:Kaiya_Adrimetrum_TDS_cover.jpg|thumb|right|Captain Kaiya Adrimetrum, commanding officer of the New Republic corvette ''FarStar''.]]
Captain [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]], [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] of the ''FarStar'', accepted Darr's offer and accompanied a delegation down to the Airfleet vessel. During the journey to Caballa [[City/Legends|City]], Darr held a formal banquet aboard the ''Maxion'' for Adrimetrum and her delegation. During the meal, Darr received word that the cruiser ''Desaclates'' was under attack from the Corsairs. Since the cruiser was carrying the ''FarStar''{{'s}} probe droid, he requested the assistance of Adrimetrum's starfighters since he realized that they were more advanced and considerably faster than the [[Skylark]] [[skyfighter]]s stationed on board his vessel. Adrimetrum agreed to his request as a gesture of good will, and Darr ordered the ''Maxion'' to make full speed for the ''Desaclates''. Arriving to find that the X-wings had succeeded in driving off the Corsairs, Darr personally commended them for their actions.<ref name="TMOE" />

===Defying the Devisors===
{{Quote|By frightening the council, Gevtes will once again deny our heritage. The Devisors have always had the knowledge of space travel and other advanced technology. If the Devisors should ever lose control of this knowledge, they will lose control of the council.|Horzao Darr|<ref name="TMOE" />}}
[[File:Eidasharden.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Eida Sharden, Horzao Darr's friend and insurgent.]]
Arriving in Caballa City, Darr escorted Adrimetrum to meet with the ruling council at the [[Ministry of Technology (Exocron)|Ministry of Technology]]. In a closed meeting, Darr learned from the [[Master Devisor]], [[Pagda Gevtes]], that the Devisors planned to keep the crew of the vessel on the planet for the rest of their lives, and to impound their [[Starship/Legends|starship]] for study. They proclaimed it was to maintain the safety and isolation of Exocron. Darr saw it for what it was; a way for the Devisors to retain their hold on power, and to increase their power base through controlling more advanced technology than was available to any potential enemies. The Devisors ordered a powerful [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] located in Caballa City to be locked onto the ''FarStar'', and slowly began to draw it down from [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]. They also decreed that Adrimetrum would be [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] for questioning. Disagreeing with the decision, especially in light of the fact that Adrimetrum had assisted in the defense of the ''Desaclates'', Darr finally listened to his inner feelings regarding the Devisors and resolved to do something to help Adrimetrum and the ''FarStar'' crew.<ref name="TMOE" />

As Adrimetrum was led away from the council, she exchanged glances with Darr. Before the council guards could lead away the rest of the ''FarStar'' delegation, Darr ordered his own [[Soldier/Legends|troops]] to secure them, and remove them from the chambers. Although the council guards were surprised, they did not question Darr's authority to do so, and allowed him to leave with the New Republic representatives. Darr took the ''FarStar'' delegation to the [[Eida Sharden's home|home]] of his friend, Eida Sharden, on the outskirts of Caballa City. Sharden introduced her guests to other members of the insurgency, who represented the political, financial and intellectual elite of the planet, and they agreed to assist in the rescue of Adrimetrum. In return for their assistance, Sharden wanted data on spaceflight and [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigational computers]] to allow the people of Exocron to re-attain their birthright. A provisional agreement was settled on, although Adrimetrum would have to authorize the release of information.<ref name="TMOE" />

===Rescuing Captain Adrimetrum===
[[File:Exocronrescue.jpg|thumb|right|150px|New Republic forces, aided by Darr, attempt to rescue their captain from the Devisors.]]
With the help of Sharden and the insurgency, Darr formulated a two-pronged assault. [[Liberation of Exocron#Assault on the tractor beam generator|One assault]] would focus on the [[Tractor beam projector/Legends|tractor beam generator]] that was locked onto the ''FarStar''; the insurgents knew of the location of the generator, and it would take the [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] of the ''FarStar''{{'s}} captured X-wings to destroy it. A [[Liberation of Exocron#Invasion of the Ministry of Technology|second assault]] would invade the Ministry of Technology and free Adrimetrum. Darr would be vital to the implementation of both plans. Contacting the ''Maxion'', Darr ordered the vessel to a rendezvous point near Sharden's home. He introduced the members of the ''FarStar'' crew who would attempt to rescue Adrimetrum from the Ministry to the [[Master Sergeant/Legends|Master Sergeant]] of the ''Maxion''{{'s}} [[cloud-jumper]] [[Regiment/Legends|regiment]], an elite unit that specialized in using [[repulsorlift personal jump pack]]s to strike at the enemy. The ''Maxion'' would be used as an insertion platform to allow a strike force to infiltrate the Ministry utilizing the jump packs.<ref name="TMOE" />

Darr, however, would not accompany the ''Maxion'' during the insertion mission. Using his authority, he delivered the New Republic pilots to the military [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] where their starfighters were being kept. Claiming to be there to inspect the fighters with the New Republic pilots to assist him, Darr passed through the troops guarding the hangar. His ruse was not suspected until a Devisor arrived at the hangar with several apprentices to study the starfighters. A firefight ensued, but the New Republic pilots prevailed and got their starfighters airborne. Both assaults succeeded, and troops from the ''Maxion'' arrived at the Ministry of Technology in time to arrest Master Devisor Gevtes, who had personally attempted to stop the ''FarStar'' crew from rescuing Adrimetrium by using a [[Pagda Gevtes' personal walker|personal walker]] against them. Following the downfall of the Devisors, Darr was personally thanked by Adrimetrum for his trust and help.<ref name="TMOE" />

===Commander of the Exocron Combined Air-Space Fleet===
{{Quote|Supreme Admiral Darr says we all in good position. Also says Airfleet ships ready if pirates get past.|[[Chin]]|<ref name="VOTF" />}}
[[File:Jorj.jpg|thumb|left|Former smuggler Jorj Car'das]]
By [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]],<ref name="ERC">The events of ''[[Vision of the Future]]'' are dated to [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> Darr had risen to the rank of Supreme Admiral,<ref name="VOTF" /> having finally accepted the post that many wanted him to ascend to earlier in his career.<ref name="TMOE" /> He was in overall command of the [[Exocron Combined Air-Space Fleet]], the successor to the Airfleet. When the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] and [[Slaver/Legends|slaver]] [[Rei'Kas]]—who coveted the planet's wealth and resources—[[Battle of Exocron|threatened]] the planet with his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], Darr delegated command of the space forces to [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Trey David]], while he readied the Airfleet ships in case Rei'Kas's forces broke through the perimeter. Darr was in contact with the former [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Jorj Car'das]], who had retired to the planet after spending over a dozen years in the company of the [[Aing-Tii/Legends|Aing-Tii]], a mysterious [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with a understanding of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Car'das had studied the Aing'Tii's philosophy and their control over the Force, and the aliens had agreed to protect him should he ever need it. Darr arranged for the orbiting fleet to pull back before engaging the enemy, subsequently drawing Rei'Kas's forces in closer to the planet. The Aing-Tii honored their agreement with Car'das, using their unique stardrives to appear behind the pirates, surprising and [[Death/Legends|destroying]] them.<ref name="VOTF" />

==Personality and traits==
Horzao Darr was a competent and able military commander who rose to command rank on ability, rather than through favors, and was versed in combat tactics regarding the deployment of air vehicles. An honorable and loyal man, Darr upheld his oath to the Airfleet, even if it meant supporting a regime that he personally felt was denying the people of Exocron their birthright. He resisted attempts to promote him to a flag rank for several years, preferring to remain as an airship commander. Darr was not without his doubts, though, and held reservations about the Devisors. He also knew of several insurgents, but did not reveal their identities, instead distancing himself from them since he deemed their views "too radical." It took the harsh decision of the Devisors concerning the crew of the ''FarStar'' to finally make him turn on the Devisors and support the insurgents. Captain Kaiya Adrimetrum had risked members of her crew to help defend the ''Desaclates'', and Darr felt that she and her people had been treated unfairly. Darr was also a practical man, as he realized that the best chance to bring down the Devisors lay with the ''FarStar'' and its superior technology. Darr wore sideburns that reached his jawline, and appeared to many to be born to wear a uniform.<ref name="TMOE" />

==Skills and abilities==
Trained in personal defense, Horzao Darr was experienced in [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee combat]] and in the use of [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrower]] weapons and their maintenance and repair. He also had training with [[Missile/Legends|missile]] weapons, and [[Medic/Legends|first aid]]. Darr could operate hover and [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles, and was able to perform repairs on the latter. He was proficient in the use of the communications and security systems in use on Exocron, and had a wide knowledge of the [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]] and culture of his homeworld. He was also knowledgeable about the [[Exocron system/Legends|Exocron system]], despite never having left the planet by 8 ABY.<ref name="TMOE" />

==Equipment==
Horzao Darr dressed in the ornate [[uniform]] of the Exocron Airfleet that befitted his rank, which included a high collar, shoulder boards, and a sash. He always carried a slugthrower pistol, ceremonial [[Sword/Legends|sword]] and [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] while on duty. Darr [[Smoking/Legends|smoked]] a [[steam pipe]], and kept one inside his uniform tunic. He puffed on the [[Pipe/Legends|pipe]] while listening to the New Republic pilots who landed on the ''Maxion''.<ref name="TMOE" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Katholoutback.jpg|thumb|right|The cover for the RPG supplement ''The Kathol Outback''.]]
Horzao Darr first appeared in the [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] adventure ''[[The Masters of Exocron]]'', which was a part of the ''[[The Kathol Outback]]'' supplement<ref name="KO">''[[The Kathol Outback]]''</ref> published on [[January 15]], [[1996]].<ref name="BN">{{BN|url=w/star-wars-the-roleplaying-game-west-end-games-incorporated/1018175964|text=Star Wars the Roleplaying Game: Darkstryder Campaign: Kathol Outback}}</ref> ''The Kathol Outback'' was part of ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign (series)|The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a multi-book adventure published by [[West End Games]]. ''The Masters of Exocron'' was written by [[Tony Russo]] and illustrated by [[Storn Cook]].<ref name="KO" /> Horzao Darr's only other appearance within ''[[Star Wars]]'' literature were several references to the character in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s novel ''[[Vision of the Future]]''.<ref name="VOTF" /> Zahn had collaborated on parts of ''The DarkStryder Campaign'', contributing the story ''[[The Saga Begins]]'' to the [[The DarkStryder Campaign|first book]] of the series.<ref name="TDC">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]''</ref>

==Appearances==
